# RustyNES v2.0.3 — "Harbor"

**The third release of the v2.0.x "Harbor" train — and the one that makes the
octal-latch accuracy work real at the shipped default.** The 2-cycle-ALE PPU
fetch model that v2.0.2 verified behind an experimental flag is **promoted to the
unconditional default and only fetch path** (ADR 0030), so the shipped build now
scores **AccuracyCoin 141/141 (100.00%)** — both new upstream PPU tests, **"ALE +
Read"** (`$0491`) and **"Hybrid Addresses"** (`$0492`), pass out of the box
(previously an honest 139/141). Option 1 — the first-principles 2-cycle-ALE fetch
refactor — is fully landed.

Alongside the accuracy headline, v2.0.3 lands the **Harbor Android polish +
foss/play monetization glue** and a **host-localizable mobile bridge-warning**
API. No store submission yet (that is v2.1.0); Android continues as
GitHub-sideload.

---

## The 2-cycle-ALE fetch model, promoted (ADR 0030)

The real 2C02 PPU multiplexes its low eight VRAM address pins with the eight VRAM
data pins (PA0-7 = AD7-0); every VRAM access is a two-cycle transaction, and an
external octal latch (74LS373-class) captures A7-A0 on the ALE-high cycle and
supplies them back on the ALE-low cycle. When the address halves desync, the PPU
reads from a **hybrid address it never coherently output** — the mechanism the
two new tests exercise.

v2.0.2 shipped a whole-dot approximation of this behind the default-off
`mc-ppu-bus-addr-hybrid` flag (a `+1 coarse-X` latch-capture stand-in, exact only
for the tested alignment). v2.0.3 replaces it with a **genuine two-dot fetch**
that models each VRAM access as a real ALE-drive + read pair, so the octal latch
*naturally* carries the stale byte across dots — no reconstruction:

- **Even dot (ALE):** drive the full fetch address; load `octal_latch =
  (address & 0xFF)`. A12 / mapper notify fires here, on the real bus event.
- **Odd dot (read):** effective address `(address & 0x3F00) | octal_latch`; read
  VRAM/CHR; write the DATA byte back onto the low bus.
- **Hybrid Addresses** (`$0492`): a mid-fetch `$2006` second write during render
  stages a delayed `CopyV` (`copy_v_delay = 4`, RustyNES's fixed alignment); the
  next nametable fetch splices `{new v high $2F00}:{one-tile-ahead stale $19}` =
  `$2F19` naturally.
- **ALE + Read** (`$0491`): a mid-render `$2007` read freezes `octal_latch` on the
  read's stale DATA byte; the next pattern fetch reads `{PAR high 6}:{$FF}` =
  `$0FFF`.

Both flags — `mc-ppu-2cycle-ale` (the now-default model) and the superseded
`mc-ppu-bus-addr-hybrid` (v2.0.2's whole-dot stand-in) — are **retired**, and the
v2.0.2 stand-in code (`octal_effective`, the `OctalFetch` enum, `copy_v`, the
coarse-X reconstruction) is deleted. This follows the v2.0.0 precedent of
promoting the one-clock scheduler by deleting its flag.

## Verification — the honesty bar

- **AccuracyCoin 141/141 (100.00%, RAM-authoritative)** on the default build —
  both `$0491` and `$0492` pass; independently re-verified.
- nestest 0-diff; mmc3 A12-IRQ **18/18**; `ppu_sprites` **19/19**; `mmc1_a12`;
  blargg/kevtris all green.
- **60-ROM commercial byte-identity oracle: 60/60**, with exactly two documented
  re-blesses — **Super Mario Bros. 3** (one leftmost tile on scanline 194) and
  **Uchuu Keibitai SDF** (one tile at the MMC5 split boundary on scanline 15) now
  render *more* TriCNES-faithfully at a mid-render `$2006` scroll write; audio and
  cycle counts stay byte-identical, and no other title's output changed.
- `cargo test --workspace` green; fmt / clippy (all feature combos) /
  `RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings cargo doc` / `thumbv7em-none-eabihf` no_std
  cross-compile all clean.
- Headless frame cost rises ~10% (nestest ~4.15 ms/frame, still ~4x realtime),
  recorded in `docs/performance.md`; accepted for the accuracy gain.

## Save-state note (upgrade)

Promoting the model to the default made it the substrate for netplay rollback,
whose save/restore round-trip exposed a determinism gap: the five in-flight fetch
fields (`octal_latch`, `address_bus`, `ale_armed`, `pattern_latch_stale`,
`copy_v_delay`) were not serialized, so a mid-render checkpoint could restore them
wrong. They are now serialized in a new **`PPU_SNAPSHOT_VERSION` v5 tail** — an
**additive** change (pre-v5 `.rns` still load, upconverting to inactive rest
defaults), the same pattern the v3 / v4 tails used. All 16 netplay
rollback-determinism tests pass. A v2.0.3 save-state is **forward-incompatible**
(will not load on <= v2.0.2), but this is an in-train sub-version bump, **not** an
ADR-0028 `.rns` format-epoch break.

## Harbor Android polish + foss/play monetization glue (ADR 0025)

- **Monetization glue (step 5) wired into the `play` flavor:** a real
  `MonetizationGate` (AppLovin MAX ad glue + RevenueCat 8.10.0 entitlement wrapper),
  `feature_enabled` gating, the run-out paywall + countdown Compose overlay,
  `begin_session` at launch, `export/restore_progress` persistence, and the
  offline-grace path — all driving the Rust `AdPolicy` core from
  `rustynes-monetization`. The **`foss` flavor keeps a no-op twin**, so the default
  F-Droid artifact is behaviour-identical. Both flavors assemble; monetization is
  behaviourally **dormant** pending on-device runtime verification (v2.0.9).
- **R8 full-mode keeps** re-tuned for the monetization UniFFI package + AppLovin +
  RevenueCat; **Baseline Profile / vitals** plumbing carried forward with a
  maintainer note that on-device regeneration against the v2.0.0 core is a v2.0.9
  step (no device in the build environment).
- **Host-localizable bridge warnings:** `rustynes-mobile` gains a UniFFI-exported
  `HostWarning` enum + `drain_warning_codes()` + `host_warning_message()`;
  `drain_warnings()` is preserved verbatim, so existing hosts are unbroken.

## Dependencies

No workspace dependency changes this release (the v2.0.1 sweep carries forward).
The `play` Android flavor adds AppLovin MAX + RevenueCat via `playImplementation`
(the `foss` artifact links neither).

---

## What's next — the rest of "Harbor"

- **v2.0.4** — Android RC + the foss/play finalization-window closeout.
- **v2.0.5–v2.0.8** — iOS finalization on the v2.0.0 core.
- **v2.0.9** — joint on-device readiness verification (Baseline Profile capture,
  vitals, foldable WYSIWYG, live monetization runtime) for both apps.
- **v2.1.0** — the **joint Google Play + Apple App Store + AltStore PAL + F-Droid
  launch**.
